programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Train Cruise
programme.sub-title: The Vivid Colors of Gunma and Niigata
programme.desc: Journey from Gunma, located northwest of Tokyo, through Niigata and marvel at the colors of early summer: blue seas, verdant mountains, vibrant sculptures, pastel candies. Find your favorite color.

programme.title: Japanology Plus mini
programme.desc: Women-only trips and solo camping are some of the many ways that people enjoy camping in Japan. Camping can even serve as a method of preparing for disaster. We examine its increasing popularity.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: SAMURAI WISDOM
programme.desc: In one generation, Mori Motonari rose from a minor lord to ruler of most of western Japan despite bitter betrayal. His wisdom still echoes today.

programme.title: CINEMATIC JOURNEY
programme.desc: Daniel Moore takes a cinematic journey through Nagano. In springtime he hits the south, experiencing the breathtaking cherry blossoms, and in winter gets lost in magical winter landscapes up north.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: The Piercing Gaze
programme.sub-title: The Man with the Piercing Gaze
programme.desc: A man strikes a pose in an iconic woodblock print by the artist Sharaku. Who is the mysterious figure... and what is he doing? And why did the artist want to commemorate the moment?

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: BOSAI
programme.desc: What does it take to survive a flood? Japan has developed survival skills for floods through experiences such as typhoons. Find out how to safely navigate flooded streets and escape sinking cars.

programme.title: DESIGN MUSEUM JAPAN
programme.desc: Kikuchi Atsuki (Graphic designer) is drawn to enamelware that reflects the creators' own instincts. He talks about the appeal of designs made and used by the same person.

programme.title: KABUKI KOOL
programme.desc: Actor Kataoka Ainosuke reveals the wide variety of props used by tachiyaku, the male roles in kabuki. Explore the all-important katana sword, tobacco pipes, footwear and gold coins.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE

programme.title: Documentary 360: Unsolved Cases
programme.sub-title: Missing Bitcoins: Inside the Mt. Gox Crypto Heist
programme.desc: In 2014, Bitcoins worth 470 million USD vanished from an exchange in Tokyo. Where did they go? We delve into the growing intersection of crime and state interests in the crypto world.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Manben: Behind the Scenes of Manga
programme.desc: Hit manga artist Urasawa Naoki takes you behind the scenes of how manga is made. Honey and Clover creator Umino Chica makes her TV debut as we follow her new series, March Comes in Like a Lion!

programme.sub-title: Dietary Detox -- Zanzibar Red Colobus, Tanzania

programme.desc: This program employs "mitateru," a Japanese technique, to inspire children's imagination by encouraging them to view objects differently and develop their unique perspectives and creativity.

programme.title: BOSAI
programme.desc: What does it take to survive a flood? Japan has developed survival skills for floods through experiences such as typhoons. Find out how to safely navigate flooded streets and escape sinking cars.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climate Change the World
programme.sub-title: COP30 in Brazil: Episode 5 Regeneration Through Agroforestry
programme.desc: Episode 5 of this series on COP30 in Brazil examines agroforestry, a combination of agriculture and forestry promoted by scientists as a potential method of regenerating rainforests.

programme.title: YONEZAWA: Shirabu Onsen
programme.desc: A 700-year-old hot spring, Shirabu Onsen, is maintained via daily trips up the slopes of the 2,000-meter mountain where it sits. See the hot spring's guardian at work, in gorgeous time-lapse footage.

programme.title: Japan Railway Journal
programme.sub-title: Vertical Separation: A Key to the Survival of Local Railways
